column8: It was the beginning of a new year. Both young women were full of good spirits and happier than they__________for years. (TO BE)
column9: "I'm a little tired. All these people in the Market. I never_____________it so busy" (TO SEE)
column10: Betty said she__________enough of crowds for one day. (TO HAVE)
column11: After Maggie_________and___________her hands, she went to the child's bed. (TO WASH/TO DRY)
column12: It's lovely to see you, Gwen. I______________you a lot. (TO MISS)
column13: As soon as John____________his painting he went for a stroll. (TO FINISH)
column14: The light_____________in the last few hours and High Street looked different. (TO CHANGE)
column15: It didn't seem possible that Jane____________for a New York the day before. (TO LEAVE)
column16: When Jill___________the ironing she felt she was exhausted. (TO DO)
column17: The storm___________and a light breeze was blowing. (TO STOP)
